title: phyllanthus calciphilus
intro: Phyllanthus calciphilus, also known as the calciphilous tree or stonebreaker, is a plant species belonging to the Euphorbiaceae family, native to tropical Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and treating liver and kidney problems. Herbal preparations of Phyllanthus calciphilus are typically made from its leaves or roots, which are processed into teas, decoctions, or tinctures for consumption. The plant's extracts have also been studied for their potential in treating various diseases, including HIV, liver cirrhosis, and certain types of cancer.
